project_summary:
Bring the Hub's Aitri-integration layer (collectors, readers, version logic, compat manifest) up to the Aitri v2.0.0-rc.159 contract, so every current and future dashboard view reads correct data from projects on the 2.0.0-rc channel while the fallback path keeps serving legacy 0.1.x projects.

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI (parity/acceptance gate — this is a change to an existing system): a project on Aitri 2.0.0-rc.159 and a legacy pre-rc.41 project BOTH render correct dashboards, with zero behavioral change for the legacy path vs today.
  JTBD: 'When my projects run current Aitri, let the Hub read their real state instead of miscomputing versions and missing renamed artifacts.'
  Guardrail metric: the collection cycle for 20 registered projects stays within the parent guardrail (dashboard render ≤2s); no new reader may spawn additional processes per cycle beyond the existing aitri status --json call.

NFR category applicability: Observability and Healthcheck are covered by the parent project's NFRs (this feature adds no new server surface or endpoint); CI/CD and Security are addressed below; Performance is the guardrail above.

functional_requirements.title: Full semver parse and compare including pre-release tags
functional_requirements.description: Replace the stable-only X.Y.Z version handling in the integration layer with a full semver parse/compare that understands pre-release tags (2.0.0-alpha.N, 2.0.0-rc.N) with numeric pre-release ordering per semver precedence: 2.0.0-alpha.3 < 2.0.0-rc.15 < 2.0.0-rc.159 < 2.0.0. Applies everywhere a version is parsed, compared, or displayed: aitri CLI version detection, per-project aitriVersion, snapshot eligibility gate (MIN vs detected), VERSION_MISMATCH alert, integration-review baseline compare, and dashboard display. Zero-dependency hand-rolled comparator.
acceptance_criteria: compare('2.0.0-rc.159','2.0.0-rc.15') returns >0 (numeric, not lexicographic, ordering of the pre-release counter)
compare('2.0.0-rc.159','2.0.0') returns <0 (a pre-release precedes its stable release)
compare('2.0.0-alpha.27','2.0.0-rc.1') returns <0 (alpha precedes rc)
Detected version '2.0.0-rc.159' is stored and displayed with its full tag — no truncation to '2.0.0' anywhere in dashboard.json or the UI
An unparseable version string (e.g. 'git-deadbeef') is rejected by the parser and the caller degrades exactly as it does today for a missing version (no crash, no fabricated compare result)

functional_requirements.title: Fallback readers accept post-rc.41 artifact names with pre-rc.41 fallback
functional_requirements.description: The direct-parsing fallback path reads 04_BUILD_REPORT.json and 05_TRACEABILITY.json (rc.41 names) wherever it currently reads 04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json / 05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json — the drift-detection ARTIFACT_MAP and the compliance reader. Because the fallback path exists to serve old CLIs, each read tries the new name first and falls back to the old name when the new one is absent. When BOTH names exist, the new name wins and the old file is ignored.
acceptance_criteria: A post-rc.41 project (new names on disk, no aitri CLI available) yields the same compliance summary and drift verdict as the snapshot path yields for that project
A pre-rc.41 project (old names only) yields compliance and drift output byte-identical to the current Hub release (regression parity)
A directory containing both 04_BUILD_REPORT.json and 04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json reads only the new-name file
A project with neither name present degrades exactly as today (compliance section absent, no error)

functional_requirements.title: Fallback state reader merges .aitri.local when present, tolerates absence
functional_requirements.description: Model the rc.51 state split: per-machine fields (lastSession, reconcileState, sessionContext) live in gitignored .aitri.local. The fallback reader merges .aitri.local over the shared .aitri view when the file exists. When it does not exist (every remote GitHub clone; any fresh checkout), the reader proceeds without error and simply omits those fields — it must not fabricate values from the shared file's legacy copies unless they are genuinely present there (pre-split projects).
acceptance_criteria: A local post-split project with .aitri.local present reports lastSession from .aitri.local
A remote clone (no .aitri.local) is collected with no error and no lastSession field fabricated
A pre-split legacy project (lastSession still inside .aitri) keeps reporting it exactly as today
A malformed .aitri.local (invalid JSON) is ignored with a logged warning; the shared .aitri view is still returned

functional_requirements.title: Contained-layout (layoutRoot) projects are discovered and read
functional_requirements.description: Support the rc.76 contained layout where Aitri-owned files live under an aitri/ container recorded in .aitri#layoutRoot: (a) directory scan recognizes such projects, (b) the fallback readers resolve artifact and feature paths through layoutRoot, (c) flat-layout projects (layoutRoot absent) keep resolving exactly as today.
acceptance_criteria: A contained-layout project inside a scanned directory appears on the dashboard with pipeline, tests, and compliance populated via the fallback path
A flat-layout project resolves identically to the current release (regression parity)
A .aitri declaring layoutRoot whose resolved path escapes the project root (absolute path or ../) is rejected: the project is collected as if layoutRoot were absent and a warning is logged

functional_requirements.title: Corrupt BUGS.json is visibly distinguished from zero bugs
functional_requirements.description: Consume the rc.158 status --json field bugs.parseErrors: when present and non-empty, the project record carries the parse-error scopes and the alert engine emits a warning-severity alert ('BUGS.json unreadable in <scope> — bugs are NOT counted'). The fallback bugs reader gains the same distinction: a BUGS.json that exists but fails to parse (bad syntax or non-contract shape) is reported as a parse error, not as zero bugs.
acceptance_criteria: A project whose BUGS.json contains invalid JSON shows a warning alert naming the scope, and its bug count renders as unknown/warning — not as '0 bugs'
A project with a valid empty BUGS.json ({"bugs":[]}) shows zero bugs and NO parse-error alert
A snapshot reporting bugs.parseErrors: [] produces no alert and no field noise in dashboard.json
The fallback path flags a BUGS.json with a non-contract shape (e.g. {"bugs":42}) as a parse error

functional_requirements.title: Unbound test results are surfaced as an indicator
functional_requirements.description: Consume the rc.148 snapshot surface resultsBinding: when the snapshot reports the 04_TEST_RESULTS.json is not bound to a real verify run (absent or stale binding), the project record carries that state and the card shows a warning indicator ('results not bound to a verify run'). Snapshot-only — the fallback path does not re-derive binding (it has no hash authority).
acceptance_criteria: A snapshot whose resultsBinding reports unbound/stale results yields a per-project warning indicator in dashboard.json and on the project card
A snapshot with a valid binding shows no indicator
A snapshot from an older CLI without the resultsBinding field produces no indicator and no error (absent ≠ unbound)

functional_requirements.title: Integration review baseline records full pre-release versions; rc.1→rc.159 review performed
functional_requirements.description: The integration-review machinery (compat manifest, changelog drift check, integrationAlert) operates on full pre-release versions: 'aitri-hub integration review 2.0.0-rc.159' stores reviewedUpTo:'2.0.0-rc.159' and the banner logic compares with FR-040 semantics. The hardcoded FALLBACK_BASELINE and snapshot floor remain valid floors but are compared with the new comparator. As part of shipping this feature, the actual review of Aitri's integration CHANGELOG entries rc.1→rc.159 (including all 9 breaking entries) is performed and its outcome recorded in the manifest's reviewerNote.
acceptance_criteria: integration review 2.0.0-rc.159 writes reviewedUpTo:'2.0.0-rc.159' (full tag) to integration-compat.json
With reviewedUpTo:'2.0.0-rc.159' and CLI 2.0.0-rc.159 detected, no integrationAlert fires
With reviewedUpTo:'2.0.0-rc.159' and CLI 2.0.0-rc.160 detected, the integrationAlert fires (a NEWER unreviewed pre-release is detected as newer)
integration review rejects a version string that fails the FR-040 parser with a usage error

functional_requirements.title: Snapshot projection preserves quality-gate and coverage surfaces for downstream views
functional_requirements.description: The snapshot-to-record projection carries through, when present in aitri status --json: quality_gates results (gate name + pass/fail), ac_coverage, and the coverage/requirements audit freshness fields the rc.159 STATUS_JSON contract documents — so Layer-1/2 UI features can render them without another collector change. Each field is additive and absent-tolerant. Display in this feature is limited to what already exists; no new UI section. validate --json is explicitly NOT invoked (see no_go_zone — per-cycle process budget); the QA Workspace feature adds it deliberately.
acceptance_criteria: A snapshot containing quality-gate results appears in the project's dashboard.json record with gate name and pass/fail preserved
A snapshot containing ac_coverage or coverage-audit freshness fields carries them into the record unchanged
A snapshot without these fields adds no field to the record (additive, absent-tolerant)

user_personas.goal: Trust the dashboard's bug counts, test verdicts, and deploy-readiness for audit decisions
user_personas.pain_point: A corrupt bug file or unbound test results currently render as a clean project — the dashboard can show green on data that proves nothing

user_personas.goal: Monitor projects on the current Aitri rc channel and legacy projects side by side
user_personas.pain_point: Version logic truncates rc tags so upgrade state is invisible, and post-rename projects lose compliance/drift data on the fallback path

user_personas.goal: Review Aitri contract changes once, record the baseline, and silence the banner honestly
user_personas.pain_point: The review baseline cannot express '2.0.0-rc.159', so the reviewed-up-to gate miscomputes against every rc release

user_stories.i_want: the Hub to understand pre-release Aitri versions
user_stories.so_that: version mismatch and eligibility decisions are computed against the version actually installed

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with .aitri aitriVersion '2.0.0-rc.15' and CLI 'aitri --version' reporting 2.0.0-rc.159
acceptance_criteria.then: the VERSION_MISMATCH indicator reports '2.0.0-rc.15' vs '2.0.0-rc.159' with both full tags visible

acceptance_criteria.given: a version string 'git-deadbeef' returned by a broken CLI shim
acceptance_criteria.then: parsing fails cleanly and the project degrades to the same 'version unknown' path as a missing CLI today, with no crash and no comparison performed

user_stories.i_want: post-rc.41 projects to render compliance and drift on the fallback path
user_stories.so_that: a project without a working aitri CLI still shows correct build/traceability state

acceptance_criteria.given: a project directory with spec/04_BUILD_REPORT.json and spec/05_TRACEABILITY.json and no aitri CLI on PATH
acceptance_criteria.then: the compliance summary is populated from 05_TRACEABILITY.json and phase-4/5 drift detection hashes the new-name files

acceptance_criteria.given: a legacy project with only spec/05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json
acceptance_criteria.then: the compliance summary is identical to the current Hub release's output for that project

user_stories.i_want: remote clones without .aitri.local to collect cleanly
user_stories.so_that: GitHub-registered projects render without errors or fabricated session data

acceptance_criteria.given: a cloned remote project whose .aitri has no lastSession and no .aitri.local exists
acceptance_criteria.then: collection succeeds and the record contains no lastSession field

acceptance_criteria.given: a local project with .aitri.local containing lastSession {event:'verify-run'}

user_stories.so_that: new-generation Aitri projects appear on the dashboard like flat ones

acceptance_criteria.given: a scanned directory containing a project whose .aitri declares layoutRoot 'aitri'
acceptance_criteria.when: the scan and fallback collection run
acceptance_criteria.then: the project appears with pipeline and compliance data resolved under aitri/
layoutRoot is ignored, a warning is logged, and no path outside the project root is opened

user_stories.i_want: corrupt bug files to be visibly flagged
user_stories.so_that: I never sign off a project whose '0 bugs' actually means 'unreadable bug file'

acceptance_criteria.then: a warning alert names the corrupt scope and the bug pill shows an unknown/warning state, not '0'
the project shows zero open bugs and no parse-error alert

user_stories.i_want: to see when test results are not bound to a real verify run
user_stories.so_that: I can reject evidence that was never produced by the verification spine

acceptance_criteria.given: a snapshot whose resultsBinding reports the results file is unbound
acceptance_criteria.then: the card shows a 'results not bound to a verify run' warning indicator

acceptance_criteria.given: a snapshot from an older CLI with no resultsBinding field
acceptance_criteria.then: no binding indicator appears and no error is logged

user_stories.i_want: to record the reviewed contract baseline as a full rc version
user_stories.so_that: the integration banner fires only on genuinely unreviewed releases

acceptance_criteria.given: integration-compat.json with reviewedUpTo '2.0.0-rc.159' and detected CLI 2.0.0-rc.159
acceptance_criteria.then: no integrationAlert is present in dashboard.json
it exits non-zero with a usage message and integration-compat.json is unchanged

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Snapshot-first collection is never demoted: a snapshot-eligible project is collected via aitri status --json, and the fallback readers run only when the snapshot path is ineligible or fails (existing FR-017 semantics).
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Integration test: a snapshot-eligible fixture project is collected with zero fallback-reader invocations (verified by reader instrumentation or by feeding it artifacts that would change the result if the fallback ran).

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Legacy fallback rendering is unchanged: a pre-rc.41 fixture project (old artifact names, pre-split .aitri, flat layout) produces a dashboard record equal to the current release's output for pipeline, tests, bugs, compliance, and drift.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Golden-fixture test: the legacy fixture's collected record matches the recorded pre-change golden output field-for-field (excluding timestamps).

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The localhost-only admin API and project registry CRUD keep working unchanged: non-loopback requests still receive 403; GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/projects behave as today.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing admin-api e2e suite passes without modification to its assertions.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Remote project sync keeps working unchanged: clone/pull into ~/.aitri-hub/cache and the poller backoff on 429 behave as today.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Existing remote-sync tests pass without modification to their assertions.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: dashboard.json remains additive: every field written by the current release is still written with the same type and meaning; new fields are additions only (the SPA is the Hub's own downstream consumer).
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Schema-diff test: the collected record for an unchanged fixture contains at least all current-release fields with unchanged types.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Path resolution through .aitri-declared fields (layoutRoot, artifactsDir) is confined to the project root: absolute paths, .. traversal, and symlinks escaping the root are rejected before any file read.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Unit tests: layoutRoot values '../../etc', '/etc', and a symlink pointing outside the project root each result in the field being ignored with a warning and zero reads outside the root.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The full declared test suite (unit + integration + web + e2e) runs on every push to main and includes the new reader/version/alert tests added by this feature.
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: CI workflow executes npm run test:all (or its equivalent staged jobs) and fails the build on any new-test failure.

no_go_zone: No new UI views or tabs — the overview restyle and QA Workspace are separate features (Layers 1–2 of idea_context/UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md); this feature only makes the data layer correct and adds at most inline warning indicators on existing surfaces
No GitHub API metrics (velocity/PRs), no dependency scanning, no runtime monitoring — cut from v1 by owner decision 2026-07-05
No full rendering of coverage_map, ac_coverage, or validate --json — the QA Workspace feature consumes them; this feature carries through what the snapshot already returns (FR-047) and nothing more

No validate --json invocation in the collection cycle — it would add a second per-project process per cycle, breaking the process-budget constraint; the QA Workspace feature introduces it deliberately (on-demand, not per-cycle). Explicit decision resolving audit GAP-1
No changes to Aitri Core — the Hub is a passive consumer of the documented contract; anything the contract lacks becomes feedback to Core, not a workaround
No aitri command execution beyond the existing read-only status --json / --version calls — no upgrades, approvals, or writes to consumer projects from the Hub
constraints: Zero new runtime dependencies (Hub runtime is Node built-ins only; semver comparator is hand-rolled)
Collection cycle must not spawn additional per-project processes beyond the existing aitri status --json call

idea_provenance_sources.problem: Owner requested the rc.159 catch-up in chat (2026-07-05); each gap verified by code inspection and cited in FEATURE_IDEA.md
idea_provenance_sources.users: Owner stated the target roles (QA testers/auditors primary; BA/PO/PM) in chat (2026-07-05)
idea_provenance_sources.baseline: Read from the code and ~/.aitri-hub/integration-compat.json (reviewedUpTo alpha.3-era, FALLBACK_BASELINE '0.1.80', snapshot floor '0.1.77')
idea_provenance_sources.success_metric: Given/When/Then criteria authored by the agent from the owner-approved scope; the specific thresholds were not individually ratified by the owner
idea_provenance_sources.no_go_zone: Owner's explicit v1 scope decision in chat (2026-07-05): Aitri-data-only, UI layers separate

idea_gaps: success_metric: the concrete acceptance scenarios in FEATURE_IDEA.md were derived by the agent from the owner-approved plan, not individually confirmed — owner ratifies or corrects them at approve 1

coverage_map.need: Pre-release semver parsed/compared/displayed correctly across the integration layer
Fallback drift detection (ARTIFACT_MAP) reads post-rc.41 artifact names
Fallback compliance reader reads 05_TRACEABILITY.json with legacy fallback
Per-machine state split (.aitri.local) merged when present, tolerated when absent
Contained layout (layoutRoot) supported in directory scan
Contained layout supported in fallback artifact/feature path resolution
Corrupt BUGS.json distinguishable from zero bugs (snapshot bugs.parseErrors + fallback equivalent)
Unbound test results (resultsBinding) surfaced as indicator
quality_gates results carried through the snapshot projection for future views
ac_coverage and coverage-audit freshness fields carried through the snapshot projection (data layer can read them)
validate --json read by the data layer
Review baseline records full pre-release version; review command validates input
Actual review of integration CHANGELOG rc.1→rc.159 (9 breaking entries) performed and recorded
Fossil baselines (FALLBACK_BASELINE, snapshot floor) compared with pre-release-aware comparator
validate --json / coverage_map / ac_coverage full rendering
New UI views for QA roles
Snapshot-first collection order preserved
Legacy project rendering unchanged
Admin API and registry CRUD unchanged
Remote sync behavior unchanged
dashboard.json shape stays additive for the SPA
Path fields from untrusted .aitri confined to project root

original_brief:
## Feature
Bring the Hub's Aitri-integration layer (collectors, readers, version logic, compat
manifest) up to the Aitri v2.0.0-rc.159 contract.

## Problem / Why
The Hub's readers were last reviewed at the 2.0.0-alpha.3 era (compat manifest,
2026-04-24). Since then Aitri Core published ~60 integration-contract entries, 9 of
them breaking. Concretely verified in the code (2026-07-05):

1. **Pre-release semver truncated** — the integration-layer version regexes accept
   stable `X.Y.Z` only and silently drop `-rc.N`/`-alpha.N`, so `2.0.0-rc.159` reads
   as `2.0.0`. Version compare, the snapshot eligibility gate, and the review
   baseline all miscompute against the rc channel the ecosystem actually runs.
   (Already a P2 in BACKLOG.md.)
2. **Fallback readers use pre-rc.41 artifact names** — `aitri-reader.js` ARTIFACT_MAP
   and the compliance reader read `04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json` /
   `05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json`; renamed rc.41 to `04_BUILD_REPORT.json` /
   `05_TRACEABILITY.json`. The fallback path exists to serve OLD CLIs, so readers
   must accept both names (new first, old fallback), not just swap.
3. **`.aitri`/`.aitri.local` split (rc.51) not modeled** — per-machine fields
   (`lastSession`, `reconcileState`, `sessionContext`) moved to gitignored
   `.aitri.local`; the fallback reader reads them from `.aitri` only. Remote clones
   never have `.aitri.local`; readers must merge when present, tolerate absence.
4. **Contained layout (rc.76 `layoutRoot`) unhandled** — directory scan and fallback
   readers assume the flat layout.
5. **New machine surfaces not consumed** — `status --json` `bugs.parseErrors`
   (corrupt BUGS.json currently indistinguishable from zero bugs), `resultsBinding`
   (results not bound to a real verify run), `quality_gates` results;
   `validate --json` now has a contract doc (VALIDATE_JSON.md).
6. **Baselines are fossils** — `reviewedUpTo` at alpha.3 era, `FALLBACK_BASELINE
   '0.1.80'`, snapshot floor `'0.1.77'` — all predating the 2.0.0-rc channel.

Every future Hub view (QA Workspace, overview restyle — see
`idea_context/UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md`) reads through this layer; it must be correct first.

## Target Users
Existing Hub users (the QA/PM/PO/BA roles monitoring Aitri projects) on projects
managed with current Aitri (2.0.0-rc channel). Also protects users with legacy
0.1.x projects, which the fallback path must keep serving.

## New Behavior
- The system must parse and compare full semver including pre-release tags
  (`2.0.0-rc.159` > `2.0.0-rc.15` > `2.0.0-alpha.3`; numeric rc/alpha ordering, not
  lexicographic) everywhere a version is compared or displayed.
- The system must read `04_BUILD_REPORT.json` and `05_TRACEABILITY.json` in the
  fallback path, falling back to the pre-rc.41 names when the new names are absent.
- The system must merge `.aitri.local` into the fallback state view when the file
  exists and degrade gracefully (no error, fields absent) when it does not.
- The system must discover and read projects using the contained layout
  (`.aitri#layoutRoot`) in both the directory scan and the fallback readers.
- The system must surface `bugs.parseErrors` from `status --json` as a per-project
  warning (corrupt BUGS.json must be distinguishable from zero bugs).
- The system must surface `resultsBinding` absence/staleness as a per-project
  indicator when the snapshot provides it.
- The system must record the integration review baseline as a full pre-release
  version (e.g. `2.0.0-rc.159`) in `integration-compat.json`, and the review of the
  rc.1→rc.159 CHANGELOG (including its 9 breaking entries) must be performed and
  recorded as part of this feature.

## Success Criteria
- Given a project on Aitri 2.0.0-rc.159, when the Hub collects it via the snapshot
  path, then version displays/compares show the full rc tag and no spurious
  version-mismatch or integration alerts fire after `integration review 2.0.0-rc.159`.
- Given a legacy project (pre-rc.41 artifact names, old CLI), when collected via
  the fallback path, then pipeline/tests/compliance/drift render exactly as before
  (no regression).
- Given a post-rc.41 project WITHOUT a working `aitri` CLI (fallback path), when
  collected, then compliance and drift read the new artifact names correctly.
- Given a project whose BUGS.json is corrupt, when collected, then the dashboard
  shows a parse-error warning, not "0 bugs".
- Given a remote (GitHub) project with no `.aitri.local`, when collected, then no
  error and no fabricated lastSession appears.

## Touch Points
Modifies existing modules (no new commands): `lib/collector/aitri-reader.js`
(ARTIFACT_MAP, state fields), `lib/collector/compliance-reader.js`,
`lib/collector/snapshot-reader.js` (projection of new fields),
`lib/collector/index.js` (eligibility gate), `lib/utils/scan.js` (contained layout),
`lib/integration/` version regexes + `lib/store/compat-manifest.js` +
`lib/constants.js` (baselines), `lib/alerts/engine.js` (new alert rules),
`web/src` ProjectCard/alert surfaces for the two new indicators (display only —
[ASSUMPTION] minimal display of parseErrors/resultsBinding belongs here rather than
waiting for the UI layers, because invisible data has no consumer; confirmed
direction with owner 2026-07-05, exact placement is implementer's call).

## Must Not Break (Regression Boundary)
- Snapshot-first collection: a snapshot-eligible project must still be collected
  via `aitri status --json`, never demoted to fallback (FR-017 semantics).
- Legacy fallback rendering: a pre-rc.41 project renders pipeline, tests, bugs,
  compliance, and drift identically to today.
- Localhost-only admin API and project registry CRUD keep working unchanged.
- Remote project sync (clone/pull cache, poller backoff) keeps working unchanged.
- Dashboard write cycle: `dashboard.json` keeps its shape — all additions to the
  Hub's own output are additive (Hub has its own downstream consumer: the SPA).

## Out of Scope
- All new UI views (overview restyle, QA Workspace tabs) — Layers 1–2, separate
  features per `idea_context/UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md`.
- GitHub API metrics (velocity/PRs), dependency scanning, runtime monitoring — cut
  from v1 by owner decision (2026-07-05).
- `coverage_map`/`ac_coverage`/`validate --json` full rendering — consumed by the
  QA Workspace feature; this feature only ensures the data layer can read them.
